normalize: P1: No <header> semantic element wrapping the navigation — nav exists but is not inside a <header> tag, hurting screen reader landmark navigation
P2: Skip-to-content link uses transition on 'top' property which may cause layout shift when triggered
P2: Section numbering labels (01, 02, 03, etc.) are decorative but not marked aria-hidden, adding noise for screen readers
P2: The 'RE' logo link in nav has minimal accessible text — just two letters, no expanded aria-label distinguishing it from the other home link
harden: P0: ALL 4 external GitHub links return HTTP 404 — the repository https://github.com/RafayelGardishyan/rafayels-engineering does not exist or is private. This breaks the primary 'View on GitHub', 'Documentation', 'Issues', and agents links
P1: No focus-visible outline styles detected on links (outline-style: none) — keyboard users cannot see which element is focused
P2: Copy buttons (e35, e36) have no visible feedback mechanism confirming the copy succeeded — no toast, no icon change observable
P2: No error boundaries or fallback content if JavaScript fails to load (animations depend on JS)
optimize: P2: HTML payload is ~56KB which is moderate for a landing page but acceptable
P2: 10 active CSS animations running simultaneously — may cause jank on low-end devices
P3: Inline SVG favicon could be extracted to a file for caching
P3: Two stylesheets and two scripts — reasonable but could be bundled
adapt: P1: No responsive testing performed, but viewport meta tag is present. Need to verify mobile breakpoints actually work
P2: H1 at 96px font-size may overflow on narrow viewports if not properly scaled down
P2: prefers-reduced-motion is not being matched (returned false) but no evidence of reduced-motion media query implementation to disable 10 running animations
clarify: P2: Hero subtext is dense — 'One command takes you from idea to merged PR — with automated code review, architecture records, and knowledge that compounds' is a long value proposition that could be broken up
P2: 'Three Commands to Ship' section doesn't clearly explain what each command does to a first-time visitor
P3: Footer links are terse ('GitHub', 'Documentation', 'Issues') — no context about what documentation covers or what issues to report

findings: P2: Hover states exist with 0.2s transitions on all interactive elements — good baseline
P1: Focus-visible states are MISSING (outline-style: none on all links) — keyboard accessibility is broken. Users tabbing through the page cannot see which element is active. /harden fix required
P2: Nav links use color 0.2s transition on hover — functional but subtle. Could benefit from underline or background change for clearer affordance. /polish
P2: Primary CTA 'Install Plugin' has background + transform + box-shadow transitions — well-implemented hover state
P2: Secondary CTA 'See How It Works' has border-color + color + transform transitions — appropriate differentiation from primary
P3: The '+21 more agents' link has border-color + color + background transitions — visually consistent with secondary actions

findings.description: ALL 4 external GitHub links return 404. The repository at github.com/RafayelGardishyan/rafayels-engineering does not exist or is private. Every external link on the page is broken — the 'View on GitHub' CTA, footer links to Documentation and Issues, and the agents link.
findings.recommendation: Either make the GitHub repository public, or update all hrefs to point to the correct repository URL. This is the single most critical issue on the page.

findings.description: No focus-visible outlines on any interactive elements. Keyboard users cannot see which element is focused when tabbing through the page. outline-style is 'none' on all links.
findings.recommendation: Add :focus-visible styles with a visible outline (e.g., 2px solid #3877e0 with 2px offset) to all interactive elements. This is a WCAG 2.1 AA failure.

findings.description: No <header> semantic HTML element. The nav bar floats without a proper landmark container, which makes it harder for assistive technology to identify the page header region.
findings.recommendation: Wrap the nav element in a <header> element for proper semantic structure.

findings.description: No prefers-reduced-motion media query detected while 10 CSS animations are running. Users who have indicated they prefer reduced motion will still see all animations.
Copy buttons (for install and feature commands) provide no visible feedback that content was copied. No toast notification, no icon change, no text change observed.
findings.recommendation: Add a brief 'Copied!' tooltip or checkmark icon animation that appears for 1.5s after clicking the copy button.

findings.description: H1 at 96px with line-height of 96px (1:1 ratio) creates tight leading. The two-line title 'Rafayel's Engineering' risks clipping descenders on the first line when they overlap ascenders on the second line.
findings.recommendation: Increase H1 line-height to at least 1.1 (105.6px) to provide breathing room between lines.

findings.description: The Feature Pipeline section uses a 5-column grid that may collapse poorly on tablet breakpoints. Each column has very tight text that could become unreadable at narrow widths.
findings.recommendation: Verify pipeline section stacks appropriately at 768px-1024px. Consider a horizontal scroll or accordion pattern for tablet sizes.

findings.description: The dark engineering dashboard aesthetic is well-executed but generic. It follows the standard dark SaaS landing page template without any distinctive brand personality or memorable visual moments.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding one bold, distinctive element — an animated terminal demo, a live agent visualization, or an interactive code review sample that demonstrates the product in action.

findings.description: The color palette is almost entirely monochromatic (dark navy backgrounds with blue-gray text). The only accent is the blue CTA button (#3877e0). While cohesive, the lack of color variation makes it hard to create visual hierarchy beyond size changes.
findings.recommendation: Introduce a secondary accent color (the cyan #22d3ee from the favicon could work) for highlights, badges, or section differentiators to add depth without breaking the palette.

findings.description: No error recovery path exists. If a user clicks a broken GitHub link, they hit a 404 with no way back. No custom error handling or graceful degradation.
findings.recommendation: Add target='_blank' with rel='noopener' to external links (so the page stays open), and fix the broken URLs.

findings.description: No help or documentation accessible from the page itself. The 'Documentation' footer link goes to a 404. Users have no way to learn about the plugin's capabilities beyond the landing page copy.
findings.recommendation: Create an actual documentation page or ensure the GitHub README is accessible.

findings.description: The agents section shows 8 agents in a grid with a '+21 more' link, but the grid cards lack visual borders or sufficient separation. In the dark theme they blend together.
findings.recommendation: Add subtle card borders (1px solid rgba(56, 119, 224, 0.15)) or increase gap spacing to better differentiate individual agent cards.

findings.description: The decorative section numbers (01, 02, 03, etc.) use a different visual weight than their labels but aren't aria-hidden, adding screen reader clutter.
findings.recommendation: Add aria-hidden='true' to the section number spans, or use CSS ::before pseudo-elements instead.

findings.description: The page uses Google Fonts (Schibsted Grotesk, DM Mono, Instrument Serif) which adds external dependency and potential FOUT/FOIT. No font-display strategy visible.
findings.recommendation: Add font-display: swap to @font-face declarations and consider preloading critical font files with <link rel='preload'>.

summary: A competent dark-themed engineering landing page with strong typographic choices (Instrument Serif + Schibsted Grotesk is an elegant pairing) and clean semantic structure (proper ARIA labels, single H1, logical heading hierarchy). However, the site has a critical P0 issue: every single external link is broken (all GitHub URLs return 404), making the page a dead end for users who want to actually install or explore the plugin. Accessibility is also compromised by missing focus-visible styles and absent prefers-reduced-motion support. The visual design, while polished and professional, lacks a distinctive identity that would set it apart from generic dark SaaS templates.
